payload.title: The Secret Language of Honeybees
paragraphs: A single honeybee may seem like a small and simple creature, but inside the hive it belongs to one of the most organised societies in nature. Thousands of bees must share information about where to find food, and they do this without words, maps, or any of the tools that people rely on. Instead, they have developed a remarkable form of communication that scientists are still working to fully understand.

When a bee discovers a rich patch of flowers, it returns to the hive and performs what researchers call a waggle dance. By moving in a figure-eight pattern and shaking its body, the bee tells the others both the direction of the food and how far away it lies. The angle of the dance points the way relative to the sun, while the length of the waggling run signals the distance. In this way, a few seconds of movement can carry directions that would take a person a paragraph to describe.

What makes this behaviour so striking is that it is partly learned. Young bees that watch experienced dancers make fewer mistakes once they begin to forage themselves, and they pass on what they have absorbed to the next generation. A skill once thought to be pure instinct now appears to depend, at least in part, on careful observation and practice.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Honeybees share detailed information about food through a learned dance, revealing a surprisingly complex form of communication.
Honeybees rely on simple instinct alone and cannot tell one another where food is located.
Honeybees use spoken sounds and small maps to guide each other to flowers.
Honeybees are dangerous insects that scientists prefer to avoid studying closely.

questions.before: During the waggle dance, the angle of the movement shows direction, while the length of the run

questions.prompt: ミツバチの行動が一部「学習」によることを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: one of the most organised societies in nature
moving in a figure-eight pattern and shaking its body
Young bees that watch experienced dancers make fewer mistakes

questions.sentence: In other words, the dance is a kind of map written entirely with the body.

questions.prompt: 第3段落で述べられている内容として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Young bees improve by watching skilled dancers, suggesting the dance is partly learned rather than pure instinct.
Bees are born knowing the dance perfectly and never need to watch others at all.
Older bees refuse to teach younger ones and keep the dance a secret.
Scientists have proven that the waggle dance carries no useful information.

payload.title: Gardens on the Rooftops
paragraphs: In many crowded cities, the most surprising green spaces are no longer found at street level but high above it, on the flat roofs of offices, schools, and apartment blocks. As open land grows scarce and rents climb, people have begun to look upward for the room to grow plants, and ordinary rooftops are slowly turning into small gardens and even working farms.

These gardens do far more than look pleasant. A layer of soil and leaves keeps the building below cooler in summer, which lowers the cost of running air conditioners. The plants also soak up rainwater that would otherwise rush into crowded drains, and they offer bees and birds a rare place to rest in the middle of the concrete city.

For the people who tend them, the benefits are just as real. Office workers come up during their breaks to water vegetables and share the harvest, and neighbours who once barely spoke now meet among the pots and beds. What began as a clever use of empty space has quietly become a way of bringing communities together.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Rooftop gardens make crowded cities greener and bring practical and social benefits to the people nearby.
Rooftop gardens are too expensive and difficult to build, so cities have given up on them.
Rooftop gardens are useful only for growing flowers and have no effect on buildings.
Rooftop gardens force people to move out of city centres into the countryside.

questions.before: A layer of soil and leaves keeps the building below
questions.after: in summer, which lowers cooling costs.

questions.prompt: 屋上庭園が人と人との結びつきを生むことを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: the flat roofs of offices, schools, and apartment blocks
soak up rainwater that would otherwise rush into crowded drains
neighbours who once barely spoke now meet among the pots and beds
a rare place to rest in the middle of the concrete city

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Rooftop gardens use unused space to cool buildings, help wildlife, and bring neighbours together.
Rooftop gardens are mainly a fashion that offers no real benefit to cities or people.
Rooftop gardens harm buildings and should be removed as quickly as possible.
Rooftop gardens can only be built in the countryside where land is cheap.

listeningQuestions.transcript: Would you mind sending me the updated schedule before the meeting starts?
The next train to the airport leaves from platform four at a quarter past seven.
Had I known about the delay, I would have taken an earlier bus into town.
He acts as though he understands the problem, but he keeps asking the same question.
The smooth, shallow stream wound quietly through the shaded woods near the bridge.
I'm not sure whether we should book the hotel now or wait for a better price.
